one_time_description: Agricultural Benefit Settlement received from Government of Canada; $15,164,000 allocated to Minors Trust, $18,109,999 to Future Per Capita Trust, remainder distributed to members ($38,085,973 in Settlement Distributions expense)
economic_notes: Six wholly-owned business enterprises: Sunchild Construction GP Ltd., Sunchild Gravel GP Ltd., Sunchild Store (2005) Ltd., Sunchild Oil & Gas Ltd., Sunchild Utilities Limited Partnership, Sunchild Business Development GP Corp. Accounted for under modified equity method. Agricultural Benefit Settlement and Treaty Land Entitlement claims being litigated.
notes: Large one-time Agricultural Benefit Settlement of $77,031,467 received from Government of Canada in FY2024, causing a dramatic increase in revenue and accumulated surplus. Settlement funds allocated to: Minors Trust ($15,164,000), Future Per Capita Trust ($18,109,999), and direct distributions to members ($38,085,973). Six GBEs accounted for under modified equity method (not consolidated). No schedule of remuneration for Chief, Council, or senior staff included in the financial statements. total_government_transfers estimated as ISC + Health Canada + ISET + CMHC + FNDF + GoA school funding; osr includes Other revenue, Trust Funds, Agricultural Benefit Settlement, and deferred contribution movements. Education expenses summed from School lines: Aboriginal Headstart $123,763 + GoA $701,540 + Canadian Heritage Language Grant $78,491 + Special Education $234,086 + Operations $7,608,123 + EPP $270,008 + Child and Family Services $855,383 partially; housing includes Band Owned Housing Administration $186,229 + Housing $1,435,416. Annual debt service reflects long-term liabilities repaid per cash flow statement.
